1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["pdm-backend"]
3	build-backend = "pdm.backend"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "fastapi"
7	dynamic = ["version"]
8	description = "FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production"
9	readme = "README.md"
10	license = "MIT"
11	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
12	requires-python = ">=3.10"
13	authors = [
14	    { name = "Sebastián Ramírez", email = "tiangolo@gmail.com" },
15	]
16	classifiers = [
17	    "Intended Audience :: Information Technology",
18	    "Intended Audience :: System Administrators",
19	    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
20	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
21	    "Programming Language :: Python",
22	    "Topic :: Internet",
23	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks",
24	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules",
25	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries",
26	    "Topic :: Software Development",
27	    "Typing :: Typed",
28	    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
29	    "Environment :: Web Environment",
30	    "Framework :: AsyncIO",
31	    "Framework :: FastAPI",
32	    "Framework :: Pydantic",
33	    "Framework :: Pydantic :: 2",
34	    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
35	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
36	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
37	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
38	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
39	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
40	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
41	    "Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP :: HTTP Servers",
42	    "Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP",
43	]
44	dependencies = [
45	    "starlette>=0.46.0",
46	    "pydantic>=2.9.0",
47	    "typing-extensions>=4.8.0",
48	    "typing-inspection>=0.4.2",
49	    "annotated-doc>=0.0.2",
50	]
51	
52	[project.urls]
53	Homepage = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi"
54	Documentation = "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/"
55	Repository = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi"
56	Issues = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues"
57	Changelog = "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/release-notes/"
58	
59	[project.optional-dependencies]
60	standard = [
61	    "fastapi-cli[standard] >=0.0.8",
62	    # For the test client
63	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
64	    # For templates
65	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
66	    # For forms and file uploads
67	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
68	    # To validate email fields
69	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
70	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
71	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
72	    # # Settings management
73	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
74	    # # Extra Pydantic data types
75	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
76	]
77	
78	standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli = [
79	    "fastapi-cli[standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli] >=0.0.8",
80	    # For the test client
81	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
82	    # For templates
83	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
84	    # For forms and file uploads
85	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
86	    # To validate email fields
87	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
88	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
89	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
90	    # # Settings management
91	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
92	    # # Extra Pydantic data types
93	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
94	]
95	
96	all = [
97	    "fastapi-cli[standard] >=0.0.8",
98	    # # For the test client
99	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
100	    # For templates
101	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
102	    # For forms and file uploads
103	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
104	    # For Starlette's SessionMiddleware, not commonly used with FastAPI
105	    "itsdangerous >=1.1.0",
106	    # For Starlette's schema generation, would not be used with FastAPI
107	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1",
108	    # To validate email fields
109	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
110	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
111	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
112	    # Settings management
113	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
114	    # Extra Pydantic data types
115	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
116	]
117	
118	[project.scripts]
119	fastapi = "fastapi.cli:main"
120	
121	[dependency-groups]
122	dev = [
123	    { include-group = "tests" },
124	    { include-group = "docs" },
125	    { include-group = "translations" },
126	    "playwright >=1.57.0",
127	    "prek >=0.2.22",
128	]
129	docs = [
130	    { include-group = "docs-tests" },
131	    "black >=25.1.0",
132	    "cairosvg >=2.8.2",
133	    # for MkDocs live reload
134	    "click==8.2.1",
135	    "griffe-typingdoc >=0.3.0",
136	    "griffe-warnings-deprecated >=1.1.0",
137	    "jieba >=0.42.1",
138	    "markdown-include-variants >=0.0.8",
139	    "mdx-include >=1.4.1,<2.0.0",
140	    "mkdocs-macros-plugin >=1.5.0",
141	    "mkdocs-material >=9.7.0",
142	    "mkdocs-redirects >=1.2.1,<1.3.0",
143	    "mkdocstrings[python] >=0.30.1",
144	    "pillow >=11.3.0",
145	    "python-slugify >=8.0.4",
146	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1,<7.0.0",
147	    "typer >=0.21.1",
148	]
149	docs-tests = [
150	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
151	    "ruff >=0.14.14",
152	    # For UJSONResponse
153	    "ujson >=5.8.0",
154	    # For ORJSONResponse
155	    "orjson >=3.9.3",
156	]
157	github-actions = [
158	    "httpx >=0.27.0,<1.0.0",
159	    "pydantic >=2.9.0,<3.0.0",
160	    "pydantic-settings >=2.1.0,<3.0.0",
161	    "pygithub >=2.3.0,<3.0.0",
162	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1,<7.0.0",
163	    "smokeshow >=0.5.0",
164	]
165	tests = [
166	    { include-group = "docs-tests" },
167	    "anyio[trio] >=3.2.1,<5.0.0",
168	    "coverage[toml] >=7.13,<8.0",
169	    "dirty-equals >=0.9.0",
170	    "flask >=3.0.0,<4.0.0",
171	    "inline-snapshot >=0.21.1",
172	    "mypy >=1.14.1",
173	    "pwdlib[argon2] >=0.2.1",
174	    "pyjwt >=2.9.0",
175	    "pytest >=9.0.0",
176	    "pytest-codspeed >=4.2.0",
177	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1,<7.0.0",
178	    "sqlmodel >=0.0.31",
179	    "strawberry-graphql >=0.200.0,<1.0.0",
180	    "ty>=0.0.9",
181	    "types-orjson >=3.6.2",
182	    "types-ujson >=5.10.0.20240515",
183	    "a2wsgi >=1.9.0,<=2.0.0",
184	    "pytest-xdist[psutil]>=2.5.0",
185	    "pytest-cov>=4.0.0",
186	    "pytest-sugar>=1.0.0",
187	    "pytest-timeout>=2.4.0",
188	]
189	translations = [
190	    "gitpython >=3.1.46",
191	    "pydantic-ai >=0.4.10",
192	    "pygithub >=2.8.1",
193	]
194	
195	[tool.pdm]
196	version = { source = "file", path = "fastapi/__init__.py" }
197	distribution = true
198	
199	[tool.pdm.build]
200	source-includes = [
201	    "tests/",
202	    "docs_src/",
203	    "scripts/",
204	    # For a test
205	    "docs/en/docs/img/favicon.png",
206	]
207	
208	[tool.mypy]
209	plugins = ["pydantic.mypy"]
210	strict = true
211	
212	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
213	module = "fastapi.concurrency"
214	warn_unused_ignores = false
215	ignore_missing_imports = true
216	
217	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
218	module = "fastapi.tests.*"
219	ignore_missing_imports = true
220	check_untyped_defs = true
221	
222	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
223	module = "docs_src.*"
224	disallow_incomplete_defs = false
225	disallow_untyped_defs = false
226	disallow_untyped_calls = false
227	
228	[tool.pytest]
229	minversion = "9.0"
230	addopts = [
231	  "--strict-config",
232	  "--strict-markers",
233	  "--ignore=docs_src",
234	]
235	strict_xfail = true
236	filterwarnings = [
237	    "error",
238	]
239	timeout = "20"
240	
241	[tool.coverage.run]
242	parallel = true
243	data_file = "coverage/.coverage"
244	source = [
245	    "docs_src",
246	    "tests",
247	    "fastapi"
248	]
249	relative_files = true
250	context = '${CONTEXT}'
251	omit = [
252	    "tests/benchmarks/*",
253	    "docs_src/response_model/tutorial003_04_py39.py",
254	    "docs_src/response_model/tutorial003_04_py310.py",
255	    "docs_src/dependencies/tutorial013_an_py310.py",  # temporary code example?
256	    "docs_src/dependencies/tutorial014_an_py310.py",  # temporary code example?
257	    # Pydantic v1 migration, no longer tested
258	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial001_an_py310.py",
259	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial002_an_py310.py",
260	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial003_an_py310.py",
261	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial004_an_py310.py",
262	]
263	
264	[tool.coverage.report]
265	show_missing = true
266	sort = "-Cover"
267	
268	[tool.coverage.html]
269	show_contexts = true

12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55

11	
12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55	
56	@router.post("/middleware")
57	def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
58	    return {"message": "OK"}

61	app.include_router(router)
62	app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)

33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

1	from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, Security
2	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
3	
4	app = FastAPI()
5	
6	counter_holder = {"counter": 0}
7	
8	
9	async def dep_counter():
10	    counter_holder["counter"] += 1
11	    return counter_holder["counter"]
12	
13	
14	async def super_dep(count: int = Depends(dep_counter)):
15	    return count
16	
17	
18	@app.get("/counter/")
19	async def get_counter(count: int = Depends(dep_counter)):
20	    return {"counter": count}
21	
22	
23	@app.get("/sub-counter/")
24	async def get_sub_counter(
25	    subcount: int = Depends(super_dep), count: int = Depends(dep_counter)
26	):
27	    return {"counter": count, "subcounter": subcount}
28	
29	
30	@app.get("/sub-counter-no-cache/")
31	async def get_sub_counter_no_cache(
32	    subcount: int = Depends(super_dep),
33	    count: int = Depends(dep_counter, use_cache=False),
34	):
35	    return {"counter": count, "subcounter": subcount}
36	
37	
38	@app.get("/scope-counter")
39	async def get_scope_counter(
40	    count: int = Security(dep_counter),
41	    scope_count_1: int = Security(dep_counter, scopes=["scope"]),
42	    scope_count_2: int = Security(dep_counter, scopes=["scope"]),
43	):
44	    return {
45	        "counter": count,
46	        "scope_counter_1": scope_count_1,
47	        "scope_counter_2": scope_count_2,
48	    }
49	
50	
51	client = TestClient(app)
52	
53	
54	def test_normal_counter():
55	    counter_holder["counter"] = 0
56	    response = client.get("/counter/")
57	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
58	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 1}
59	    response = client.get("/counter/")
60	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
61	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 2}
62	
63	
64	def test_sub_counter():
65	    counter_holder["counter"] = 0
66	    response = client.get("/sub-counter/")
67	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
68	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 1, "subcounter": 1}
69	    response = client.get("/sub-counter/")
70	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
71	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 2, "subcounter": 2}
72	
73	
74	def test_sub_counter_no_cache():
75	    counter_holder["counter"] = 0
76	    response = client.get("/sub-counter-no-cache/")
77	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
78	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 2, "subcounter": 1}
79	    response = client.get("/sub-counter-no-cache/")
80	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
81	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 4, "subcounter": 3}
82	
83	
84	def test_security_cache():
85	    counter_holder["counter"] = 0
86	    response = client.get("/scope-counter/")
87	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
88	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 1, "scope_counter_1": 2, "scope_counter_2": 2}
89	    response = client.get("/scope-counter/")
90	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
91	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 3, "scope_counter_1": 4, "scope_counter_2": 4}

1	from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware

1	from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
2	
3	from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
4	
5	
6	# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
7	# in their own AsyncExitStack
8	class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
9	    def __init__(
10	        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
11	    ) -> None:
12	        self.app = app
13	        self.context_name = context_name
14	
15	    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
16	        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
17	            scope[self.context_name] = stack
18	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

8	@app.middleware("http")
9	async def add_process_time_header(request: Request, call_next):
10	    start_time = time.perf_counter()
11	    response = await call_next(request)
12	    process_time = time.perf_counter() - start_time
13	    response.headers["X-Process-Time"] = str(process_time)
14	    return response

Now let me verify the pytest configuration and generate my report:

Based on my thorough exploration of the FastAPI test structure, here's a comprehensive report:

## Test Structure Analysis Report

### 1. **Test Application Directory Structure**

**No dedicated `tests/test_apps/` directory exists.** Instead, the FastAPI project uses these approaches:

- **Inline app definitions in test files** - Most test files define their FastAPI app directly within the test file itself
- **Documentation examples as test apps** - Tests import from `docs_src/` directory (e.g., `from docs_src.cors.tutorial001_py310 import app`)
- **Shared test app at `tests/main.py`** - A central test app used by `test_application.py`

### 2. **Middleware Test Structure**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/tests/`

**Existing middleware tests found:**
- **`tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`** - Custom ASGI middleware test (ContentSizeLimitMiddleware)
- **`tests/test_tutorial/test_cors/test_tutorial001.py`** - CORS middleware tests with OPTIONS and GET requests
- **`tests/test_tutorial/test_advanced_middleware/`** - Three middleware test files:
  - `test_tutorial001.py` - HTTP to HTTPS redirect middleware
  - `test_tutorial002.py` - Host validation middleware  
  - `test_tutorial003.py` - (location validated)

### 3. **Testing Patterns Used**

**Primary Framework:** pytest (version >=9.0.0)

**Standard Test Client:** `fastapi.testclient.TestClient`

**Common testing features:**
- pytest parametrization (`@pytest.mark.parametrize`)
- Inline snapshot testing (`inline-snapshot` package)
- xdist for distributed testing
- pytest markers (custom groups like `@workdir_lock`)
- Coverage tracking (pytest-cov)

**Example pytest configuration from `pyproject.toml`:**
```toml
[tool.pytest]
minversion = "9.0"
addopts = [
  "--strict-config",
  "--strict-markers",
  "--ignore=docs_src",
]
timeout = "20"
```

### 4. **Testing Conventions & Patterns**

**Import Patterns (standard across all test files):**
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse
```

**App Creation Patterns:**

**Pattern A - Inline minimal app:**
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()

@app.get("/")
def read_root():
    return {"message": "Hello"}

client = TestClient(app)

def test_something():
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200
```

**Pattern B - Import from docs_src:**
```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from docs_src.cors.tutorial001_py310 import app

client = TestClient(app)

def test_cors():
    response = client.get("/", headers={"Origin": "https://localhost.tiangolo.com"})
    assert response.status_code == 200
```

**Pattern C - Complex app with dependencies/handlers:**
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI(
    exception_handlers={
        HTTPException: custom_handler,
        RequestValidationError: validation_handler,
    }
)

@app.get("/endpoint")
def endpoint(dep: int = Depends(some_dependency)):
    return {"result": dep}

client = TestClient(app)
```

**Pattern D - Middleware testing with TestClient options:**
```python
client = TestClient(app, base_url="https://testserver")
client = TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)
response = client.get("/", follow_redirects=False)
```

### 5. **Shared Test Utilities**

**No global conftest.py at tests/ root level** (checked - none exists at `/tests/conftest.py`)

**Shared utility file:** `/tests/utils.py`
Contains:
- `needs_py310` - pytest marker for Python 3.10+ requirement
- `needs_py314` - pytest marker for Python 3.14+ requirement
- `workdir_lock` - xdist group marker for serialized tests
- `skip_module_if_py_gte_314()` - Module-level skip function

**Other utilities per test type:**
- `tests/test_request_params/test_form/utils.py` - Helper for extracting OpenAPI body model names

**Conftest exists only in specialized locations:**
- `/scripts/tests/test_translation_fixer/conftest.py` - Translation testing conftest

### 6. **Key Files & Paths**

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/tests/test_application.py` | Main application test suite (imports from `tests/main.py`) |
| `/tests/main.py` | Shared test FastAPI app with 100+ routes |
| `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py` | Middleware exception handling test |
| `/tests/test_tutorial/test_cors/` | CORS middleware tests |
| `/tests/test_tutorial/test_advanced_middleware/` | Complex middleware patterns |
| `/tests/test_exception_handlers.py` | Exception handler patterns |
| `/tests/test_dependency_cache.py` | Dependency injection test patterns |
| `/tests/utils.py` | Shared pytest utilities and markers |
| `/docs_src/cors/tutorial001_py310.py` | Example CORS app (tested) |
| `/docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py` | Example middleware app |

### 7. **Test App Organization Summary**

The project uses **documentation examples as the primary source of test apps** rather than dedicated `test_apps/` directories. This approach:
- Ensures documentation examples are always tested
- Prevents code duplication
- Keeps examples executable as actual tests
- Uses `tests/test_tutorial/` to organize test suites by feature area

Each major feature (CORS, advanced middleware, authentication, etc.) has a corresponding:
1. Example in `docs_src/` 
2. Test in `tests/test_tutorial/{feature}/`

This is a documentation-first testing strategy that maintains consistency between examples and functionality.

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Starlette Middleware System Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the FastAPI project (which serves as the best example of Starlette middleware usage), here's what I found:

### 1. **Starlette Installation & Dependency**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/pyproject.toml`

- **Requirement**: `starlette>=0.46.0`
- FastAPI strictly depends on Starlette as a core dependency
- All middleware implementations in FastAPI are re-exports from Starlette

### 2. **FastAPI's add_middleware() Method**

**Files**:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/fastapi/applications.py` (line 45)
- Usage examples in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/docs_src/`

**How it works**:
- `FastAPI` inherits from `Starlette` directly
- `add_middleware()` is inherited from Starlette and is NOT overridden in FastAPI
- Middleware is stored in the `user_middleware` list (line 1016-1018)
- The `middleware_stack` is lazily built when the app is first called

**Middleware Stack Order** (from `build_middleware_stack()`, lines 1022-1070):
```python
middleware = (
    [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, ...)]  # Outermost
    + self.user_middleware                     # User-added middleware
    + [
        Middleware(ExceptionMiddleware, ...),
        Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),
    ]
)
```

The middleware are applied in **reverse order** (line 1068-1069):
```python
for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
    app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
```

This means:
- **Outermost layer** (executed first): `ServerErrorMiddleware`
- **Second layer**: User middleware (in reverse order of `add_middleware()` calls)
- **Innermost layer** (executed last): `ExceptionMiddleware` and `AsyncExitStackMiddleware`
- **Core app**: `self.router`

### 3. **Middleware Contract & Implementation Patterns**

Based on the examples found, there are **three approaches**:

#### **Pattern A: Pure ASGI Middleware (Most Flexible)**
From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`:

```python
class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        
        # Wrapper pattern for receive
        async def receive_wrapper():
            message = await receive()
            # Process message
            return message
        
        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
```

**Requirements**:
- `__init__(app, *args, **kwargs)` - receives the next ASGI app and any additional parameters
- `async __call__(scope, receive, send)` - the ASGI interface
- **Scope**: Dictionary with request metadata (type, method, path, headers, client, etc.)
- **Receive**: Async callable that yields ASGI messages
- **Send**: Async callable that sends ASGI messages

#### **Pattern B: BaseHTTPMiddleware (Easier but Less Flexible)**
From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py`:

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware

@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(request: Request, call_next):
    start_time = time.perf_counter()
    response = await call_next(request)
    process_time = time.perf_counter() - start_time
    response.headers["X-Process-Time"] = str(process_time)
    return response
```

Or explicitly using `app.add_middleware()`:
```python
app.add_middleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware, dispatch=your_function)
```

**What happens internally** (from applications.py, line 4699):
- The `@app.middleware("http")` decorator wraps your function with `BaseHTTPMiddleware`
- This abstracts away the raw ASGI interface
- You work with Request and Response objects instead

#### **Pattern C: Starlette Middleware Classes (Re-exported in FastAPI)**
From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/docs_src/advanced_middleware/`:

```python
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

from fastapi.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware
app.add_middleware(GZipMiddleware, minimum_size=1000, compresslevel=5)

from fastapi.middleware.trustedhost import TrustedHostMiddleware
app.add_middleware(TrustedHostMiddleware, allowed_hosts=["example.com"])

from fastapi.middleware.httpsredirect import HTTPSRedirectMiddleware
app.add_middleware(HTTPSRedirectMiddleware)
```

### 4. **Starlette Middleware Classes in FastAPI**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/fastapi/middleware/`

All are re-exports from Starlette:
- `CORSMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.cors`
- `GZipMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.gzip`
- `HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.httpsredirect`
- `TrustedHostMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.trustedhost`
- `WSGIMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.wsgi`

**Custom FastAPI Middleware**:
- `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` - Used internally for cleaning up file handles

### 5. **How to Access Client IP Address**

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/docs_src/using_request_directly/tutorial001_py310.py`:

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request

@app.get("/items/{item_id}")
def read_root(item_id: str, request: Request):
    client_host = request.client.host  # Access client IP
    return {"client_host": client_host, "item_id": item_id}
```

**In pure ASGI middleware**:
```python
async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
    if scope["type"] == "http":
        client = scope.get("client")  # Returns tuple: (host, port)
        if client:
            host, port = client
```

### 6. **Middleware Execution Flow & ASGI Types**

**Imports from Starlette**:
```python
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
from starlette.requests import Request
from starlette.responses import Response
from starlette.middleware import Middleware
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
```

**Request/Response Flow in Pure ASGI Middleware**:
1. Middleware receives: `scope`, `receive`, `send`
2. Can wrap `receive` to intercept incoming messages
3. Can wrap `send` to intercept outgoing messages
4. Must call `await self.app(scope, receive, send)` to pass to next layer

**ASGI Message Types**:
- `scope["type"]` can be: `"http"` or `"websocket"`
- Messages from `receive()`: `{"type": "http.request", "body": bytes, ...}`
- Messages to `send()`: `{"type": "http.response.start", ...}` or `{"type": "http.response.body", ...}`

### 7. **Key Implementation Files**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/fastapi/applications.py` (lines 45, 1016-1070) | FastAPI class, middleware stack building |
| `/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py` | Context manager for resource cleanup |
| `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` | Re-exports Middleware from Starlette |
| `/fastapi/middleware/cors.py` | Re-exports CORSMiddleware from Starlette |
| `/docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py` | Basic middleware using @app.middleware("http") |
| `/docs_src/advanced_middleware/tutorial*.py` | Various Starlette middleware examples |
| `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py` | Custom pure ASGI middleware example |

### Summary: Middleware Contract

A valid Starlette middleware must:

1. **Accept initialization**: `__init__(self, app: ASGIApp, *args, **kwargs)`
2. **Be callable with ASGI signature**: `async __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send)`
3. **Call the next app**: `await self.app(scope, receive, send)` (with possibly wrapped receive/send)
4. **Handle scope type**: Typically check `if scope["type"] == "http"`
5. **Preserve the ASGI contract**: Don't consume messages improperly

**Execution Order** (request enters top, response exits bottom):
```
Request -> ServerErrorMiddleware -> UserMiddleware(s) -> ExceptionMiddleware 
        -> AsyncExitStackMiddleware -> Router -> endpoint
Response <- (reverse path)
```

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/docs_src/advanced_middleware/`:

```python

    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
```

### 4. **Starlette Middleware Classes in FastAPI**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/fastapi/middleware/`

All are re-exports from Starlette:
- `CORSMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.cors`
- `GZipMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.gzip`
- `HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.httpsredirect`
- `TrustedHostMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.trustedhost`
- `WSGIMiddleware` - from `starlette.middleware.wsgi`

**Custom FastAPI Middleware**:
- `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` - Used internally for cleaning up file handles

### 5. **How to Access Client IP Address**

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/docs_src/using_request_directly/tutorial001_py310.py`:

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request

@app.get("/items/{item_id}")
def read_root(item_id: str, request: Request):
    client_host = request.client.host  # Access client IP
    return {"client_host": client_host, "item_id": item_id}
```

**In pure ASGI middleware**:
```python
async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
    if scope["type"] == "http":
        client = scope.get("client")  # Returns tuple: (host, port)
        if client:
            host, port = client
```

### 6. **Middleware Execution Flow & ASGI Types**

**Imports from Starlette**:
```python
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
from starlette.requests import Request
from starlette.responses import Response
from starlette.middleware import Middleware
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
```

**Request/Response Flow in Pure ASGI Middleware**:
1. Middleware receives: `scope`, `receive`, `send`
2. Can wrap `receive` to intercept incoming messages
3. Can wrap `send` to intercept outgoing messages
4. Must call `await self.app(scope, receive, send)` to pass to next layer

**ASGI Message Types**:
- `scope["type"]` can be: `"http"` or `"websocket"`
- Messages from `receive()`: `{"type": "http.request", "body": bytes, ...}`
- Messages to `send()`: `{"type": "http.response.start", ...}` or `{"type": "http.response.body", ...}`

### 7. **Key Implementation Files**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/fastapi/applications.py` (lines 45, 1016-1070) | FastAPI class, middleware stack building |
| `/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py` | Context manager for resource cleanup |
| `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` | Re-exports Middleware from Starlette |
| `/fastapi/middleware/cors.py` | Re-exports CORSMiddleware from Starlette |
| `/docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py` | Basic middleware using @app.middleware("http") |
| `/docs_src/advanced_middleware/tutorial*.py` | Various Starlette middleware examples |
| `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py` | Custom pure ASGI middleware example |

### Summary: Middleware Contract

A valid Starlette middleware must:

1. **Accept initialization**: `__init__(self, app: ASGIApp, *args, **kwargs)`
2. **Be callable with ASGI signature**: `async __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send)`
3. **Call the next app**: `await self.app(scope, receive, send)` (with possibly wrapped receive/send)
4. **Handle scope type**: Typically check `if scope["type"] == "http"`
5. **Preserve the ASGI contract**: Don't consume messages improperly

**Execution Order** (request enters top, response exits bottom):
```
Request -> ServerErrorMiddleware -> UserMiddleware(s) -> ExceptionMiddleware 
        -> AsyncExitStackMiddleware -> Router -> endpoint
Response <- (reverse path)
```

1	# Middleware { #middleware }
2	
3	You can add middleware to **FastAPI** applications.
4	
5	A "middleware" is a function that works with every **request** before it is processed by any specific *path operation*. And also with every **response** before returning it.
6	
7	* It takes each **request** that comes to your application.
8	* It can then do something to that **request** or run any needed code.
9	* Then it passes the **request** to be processed by the rest of the application (by some *path operation*).
10	* It then takes the **response** generated by the application (by some *path operation*).
11	* It can do something to that **response** or run any needed code.
12	* Then it returns the **response**.
13	
14	/// note | Technical Details
15	
16	If you have dependencies with `yield`, the exit code will run *after* the middleware.
17	
18	If there were any background tasks (covered in the [Background Tasks](background-tasks.md) section, you will see it later), they will run *after* all the middleware.
19	
20	///
21	
22	## Create a middleware { #create-a-middleware }
23	
24	To create a middleware you use the decorator `@app.middleware("http")` on top of a function.
25	
26	The middleware function receives:
27	
28	* The `request`.
29	* A function `call_next` that will receive the `request` as a parameter.
30	    * This function will pass the `request` to the corresponding *path operation*.
31	    * Then it returns the `response` generated by the corresponding *path operation*.
32	* You can then further modify the `response` before returning it.
33	
34	{* ../../docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py hl[8:9,11,14] *}
35	
36	/// tip
37	
38	Keep in mind that custom proprietary headers can be added [using the `X-` prefix](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers).
39	
40	But if you have custom headers that you want a client in a browser to be able to see, you need to add them to your CORS configurations ([CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)](cors.md)) using the parameter `expose_headers` documented in [Starlette's CORS docs](https://www.starlette.dev/middleware/#corsmiddleware).
41	
42	///
43	
44	/// note | Technical Details
45	
46	You could also use `from starlette.requests import Request`.
47	
48	**FastAPI** provides it as a convenience for you, the developer. But it comes directly from Starlette.
49	
50	///
51	
52	### Before and after the `response` { #before-and-after-the-response }
53	
54	You can add code to be run with the `request`,  before any *path operation* receives it.
55	
56	And also after the `response` is generated, before returning it.
57	
58	For example, you could add a custom header `X-Process-Time` containing the time in seconds that it took to process the request and generate a response:
59	
60	{* ../../docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py hl[10,12:13] *}
61	
62	/// tip
63	
64	Here we use [`time.perf_counter()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/time.html#time.perf_counter) instead of `time.time()` because it can be more precise for these use cases. 🤓
65	
66	///
67	
68	## Multiple middleware execution order { #multiple-middleware-execution-order }
69	
70	When you add multiple middlewares using either `@app.middleware()` decorator or `app.add_middleware()` method, each new middleware wraps the application, forming a stack. The last middleware added is the *outermost*, and the first is the *innermost*.
71	
72	On the request path, the *outermost* middleware runs first.
73	
74	On the response path, it runs last.
75	
76	For example:
77	
78	```Python
79	app.add_middleware(MiddlewareA)
80	app.add_middleware(MiddlewareB)
81	```
82	
83	This results in the following execution order:
84	
85	* **Request**: MiddlewareB → MiddlewareA → route
86	
87	* **Response**: route → MiddlewareA → MiddlewareB
88	
89	This stacking behavior ensures that middlewares are executed in a predictable and controllable order.
90	
91	## Other middlewares { #other-middlewares }
92	
93	You can later read more about other middlewares in the [Advanced User Guide: Advanced Middleware](../advanced/middleware.md).
94	
95	You will read about how to handle <abbr title="Cross-Origin Resource Sharing">CORS</abbr> with a middleware in the next section.
96

1	# Advanced Middleware { #advanced-middleware }
2	
3	In the main tutorial you read how to add [Custom Middleware](../tutorial/middleware.md) to your application.
4	
5	And then you also read how to handle [CORS with the `CORSMiddleware`](../tutorial/cors.md).
6	
7	In this section we'll see how to use other middlewares.
8	
9	## Adding ASGI middlewares { #adding-asgi-middlewares }
10	
11	As **FastAPI** is based on Starlette and implements the <abbr title="Asynchronous Server Gateway Interface">ASGI</abbr> specification, you can use any ASGI middleware.
12	
13	A middleware doesn't have to be made for FastAPI or Starlette to work, as long as it follows the ASGI spec.
14	
15	In general, ASGI middlewares are classes that expect to receive an ASGI app as the first argument.
16	
17	So, in the documentation for third-party ASGI middlewares they will probably tell you to do something like:
18	
19	```Python
20	from unicorn import UnicornMiddleware
21	
22	app = SomeASGIApp()
23	
24	new_app = UnicornMiddleware(app, some_config="rainbow")
25	```
26	
27	But FastAPI (actually Starlette) provides a simpler way to do it that makes sure that the internal middlewares handle server errors and custom exception handlers work properly.
28	
29	For that, you use `app.add_middleware()` (as in the example for CORS).
30	
31	```Python
32	from fastapi import FastAPI
33	from unicorn import UnicornMiddleware
34	
35	app = FastAPI()
36	
37	app.add_middleware(UnicornMiddleware, some_config="rainbow")
38	```
39	
40	`app.add_middleware()` receives a middleware class as the first argument and any additional arguments to be passed to the middleware.
41	
42	## Integrated middlewares { #integrated-middlewares }
43	
44	**FastAPI** includes several middlewares for common use cases, we'll see next how to use them.
45	
46	/// note | Technical Details
47	
48	For the next examples, you could also use `from starlette.middleware.something import SomethingMiddleware`.
49	
50	**FastAPI** provides several middlewares in `fastapi.middleware` just as a convenience for you, the developer. But most of the available middlewares come directly from Starlette.
51	
52	///
53	
54	## `HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` { #httpsredirectmiddleware }
55	
56	Enforces that all incoming requests must either be `https` or `wss`.
57	
58	Any incoming request to `http` or `ws` will be redirected to the secure scheme instead.
59	
60	{* ../../docs_src/advanced_middleware/tutorial001_py310.py hl[2,6] *}
61	
62	## `TrustedHostMiddleware` { #trustedhostmiddleware }
63	
64	Enforces that all incoming requests have a correctly set `Host` header, in order to guard against HTTP Host Header attacks.
65	
66	{* ../../docs_src/advanced_middleware/tutorial002_py310.py hl[2,6:8] *}
67	
68	The following arguments are supported:
69	
70	* `allowed_hosts` - A list of domain names that should be allowed as hostnames. Wildcard domains such as `*.example.com` are supported for matching subdomains. To allow any hostname either use `allowed_hosts=["*"]` or omit the middleware.
71	* `www_redirect` - If set to True, requests to non-www versions of the allowed hosts will be redirected to their www counterparts. Defaults to `True`.
72	
73	If an incoming request does not validate correctly then a `400` response will be sent.
74	
75	## `GZipMiddleware` { #gzipmiddleware }
76	
77	Handles GZip responses for any request that includes `"gzip"` in the `Accept-Encoding` header.
78	
79	The middleware will handle both standard and streaming responses.
80	
81	{* ../../docs_src/advanced_middleware/tutorial003_py310.py hl[2,6] *}
82	
83	The following arguments are supported:
84	
85	* `minimum_size` - Do not GZip responses that are smaller than this minimum size in bytes. Defaults to `500`.
86	* `compresslevel` - Used during GZip compression. It is an integer ranging from 1 to 9. Defaults to `9`. Lower value results in faster compression but larger file sizes, while higher value results in slower compression but smaller file sizes.
87	
88	## Other middlewares { #other-middlewares }
89	
90	There are many other ASGI middlewares.
91	
92	For example:
93	
94	* [Uvicorn's `ProxyHeadersMiddleware`](https://github.com/encode/uvicorn/blob/master/uvicorn/middleware/proxy_headers.py)
95	* [MessagePack](https://github.com/florimondmanca/msgpack-asgi)
96	
97	To see other available middlewares check [Starlette's Middleware docs](https://www.starlette.dev/middleware/) and the [ASGI Awesome List](https://github.com/florimondmanca/awesome-asgi).
98

## Comprehensive Report: fastapi/middleware/ Directory Exploration

Based on my thorough analysis of the FastAPI middleware directory, here's a detailed breakdown of all components:

### 1. Directory Structure and Files

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/fastapi/middleware/`

**Files present:**
- `__init__.py` (58 bytes)
- `asyncexitstack.py` (637 bytes)
- `cors.py` (79 bytes)
- `gzip.py` (79 bytes)
- `httpsredirect.py` (115 bytes)
- `trustedhost.py` (109 bytes)
- `wsgi.py` (107 bytes)

### 2. __init__.py Contents

**File:** `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`

The __init__.py only contains:
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

This exports Starlette's `Middleware` class as a convenience for developers, allowing them to import it from `fastapi.middleware` instead of `starlette.middleware`.

### 3. Middleware Re-Exports (FastAPI as Convenience Layer)

FastAPI's middleware module is primarily a **convenience wrapper** that re-exports Starlette middleware classes with the `# noqa` comment (to suppress unused import warnings since these are public API exports):

| File | Content | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| `cors.py` | `from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware` | Re-export CORS middleware |
| `gzip.py` | `from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware as GZipMiddleware` | Re-export GZip compression middleware |
| `httpsredirect.py` | `from starlette.middleware.httpsredirect import HTTPSRedirectMiddleware as HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` | Re-export HTTPS redirect middleware |
| `trustedhost.py` | `from starlette.middleware.trustedhost import TrustedHostMiddleware as TrustedHostMiddleware` | Re-export Trusted Host validation middleware |
| `wsgi.py` | `from starlette.middleware.wsgi import WSGIMiddleware as WSGIMiddleware` | Re-export WSGI app wrapper middleware |

### 4. FastAPI-Specific Implementation: AsyncExitStackMiddleware

**File:** `/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py` (Only custom middleware implementation in FastAPI)

```python
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

**Key characteristics:**
- **Pattern:** Implements ASGI middleware following the callable pattern (not inheriting BaseHTTPMiddleware)
- **Purpose:** Provides cleanup/resource management after request completion, specifically for closing files after dependencies are closed
- **Context variable name:** Defaults to `"fastapi_middleware_astack"` and can be customized
- **Signature:** `__init__(app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack")`
- **Call signature:** `async __call__(scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None`
- **Behavior:** Creates an `AsyncExitStack` context manager and stores it in `scope[context_name]` so other code can register cleanup callbacks

### 5. How Middleware is Integrated into FastAPI

**Location in application:** `/fastapi/applications.py` contains the middleware orchestration

**Key integration points:**

1. **FastAPI class inheritance:**
   - `class FastAPI(Starlette)` - inherits Starlette's middleware infrastructure

2. **AsyncExitStackMiddleware integration:**
   - Imported at top: `from fastapi.middleware.asyncexitstack import AsyncExitStackMiddleware`
   - Automatically added in `build_middleware_stack()` method (lines 1022-1070)

3. **Middleware stack construction (lines 1035-1070):**
   ```python
   middleware = (
       [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]
       + self.user_middleware  # User-added middleware via add_middleware()
       + [
           Middleware(ExceptionMiddleware, handlers=exception_handlers, debug=debug),
           Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),  # FastAPI-specific
       ]
   )
   
   app = self.router
   for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
       app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
   return app
   ```

**Middleware execution order:**
- ServerErrorMiddleware (outermost - handles server errors)
- User middlewares (added via `app.add_middleware()` in LIFO order)
- ExceptionMiddleware (handles exceptions)
- AsyncExitStackMiddleware (innermost - resource cleanup)
- Router (application logic)

**Request flow:** ServerErrorMiddleware → User Middlewares → ExceptionMiddleware → AsyncExitStackMiddleware → Router
**Response flow:** Router → AsyncExitStackMiddleware → ExceptionMiddleware → User Middlewares → ServerErrorMiddleware

### 6. Middleware Class Interaction Patterns

**Two main patterns in FastAPI/Starlette:**

#### Pattern A: ASGI Callable Middleware (like AsyncExitStackMiddleware)
```python
class MyMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, ...config...):
        self.app = app
    
    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        # Pre-processing
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
        # Post-processing
```
- Low-level direct ASGI access
- Full control over request/response
- Used for custom middleware with HTTPException handling

#### Pattern B: BaseHTTPMiddleware (used by @app.middleware("http") decorator)
```python
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware

class MyHTTPMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        # Pre-processing
        response = await call_next(request)
        # Post-processing
        return response
```
- Higher-level Request/Response objects
- Automatic wrapping via decorator: `@app.middleware("http")`
- Implementation in FastAPI: line 4699 of applications.py wraps user functions in `BaseHTTPMiddleware`

### 7. Using Middleware in FastAPI

**Method 1: Using @app.middleware() decorator**
```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(request: Request, call_next):
    response = await call_next(request)
    return response
```

**Method 2: Using app.add_middleware() method**
```python
app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=["*"])
app.add_middleware(GZipMiddleware, minimum_size=1000)
```

**Method 3: Custom ASGI middleware class**
```python
class CustomMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
    
    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        # Custom processing
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)

app.add_middleware(CustomMiddleware, max_content_size=256)
```

### 8. Starlette Middleware Classes Available via FastAPI

| Middleware | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|
| `CORSMiddleware` | Cross-Origin Resource Sharing | `allow_origins`, `allow_credentials`, `allow_methods`, `allow_headers`, `expose_headers` |
| `GZipMiddleware` | GZip compression | `minimum_size` (default 500), `compresslevel` (1-9, default 9) |
| `HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` | Enforce HTTPS/WSS | None (simple toggle) |
| `TrustedHostMiddleware` | Validate Host header | `allowed_hosts` (list), `www_redirect` (bool, default True) |
| `WSGIMiddleware` | Wrap WSGI applications | WSGI app instance |

### 9. Type Signatures and ASGI Contract

**ASGI app interface:**
```python
type ASGIApp = Callable[[Scope, Receive, Send], Awaitable[None]]
```

- `Receive` - Async callable that returns messages from client
- `Send` - Async callable to send messages to client

**Middleware must implement ASGI:**
```python
async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
    # 1. Check scope["type"] == "http" or "websocket"
    # 2. Optionally wrap receive/send
    # 3. Call await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

## Comprehensive Report: fastapi/middleware/ Directory Exploration

Based on my thorough analysis of the FastAPI middleware directory, here's a detailed breakdown of all components:

### 1. Directory Structure and Files

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391106780-r1g9h7/fastapi/middleware/`

**Files present:**
- `__init__.py` (58 bytes)
- `asyncexitstack.py` (637 bytes)
- `cors.py` (79 bytes)
- `gzip.py` (79 bytes)
- `httpsredirect.py` (115 bytes)
- `trustedhost.py` (109 bytes)
- `wsgi.py` (107 bytes)

### 2. __init__.py Contents

**File:** `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`

The __init__.py only contains:
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

This exports Starlette's `Middleware` class as a convenience for developers, allowing them to import it from `fastapi.middleware` instead of `starlette.middleware`.

### 3. Middleware Re-Exports (FastAPI as Convenience Layer)

FastAPI's middleware module is primarily a **convenience wrapper** that re-exports Starlette middleware classes with the `# noqa` comment (to suppress unused import warnings since these are public API exports):

| File | Content | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| `cors.py` | `from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware` | Re-export CORS middleware |
| `gzip.py` | `from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware as GZipMiddleware` | Re-export GZip compression middleware |
| `httpsredirect.py` | `from starlette.middleware.httpsredirect import HTTPSRedirectMiddleware as HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` | Re-export HTTPS redirect middleware |
| `trustedhost.py` | `from starlette.middleware.trustedhost import TrustedHostMiddleware as TrustedHostMiddleware` | Re-export Trusted Host validation middleware |
| `wsgi.py` | `from starlette.middleware.wsgi import WSGIMiddleware as WSGIMiddleware` | Re-export WSGI app wrapper middleware |

### 4. FastAPI-Specific Implementation: AsyncExitStackMiddleware

**File:** `/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py` (Only custom middleware implementation in FastAPI)

```python
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

**Key characteristics:**
- **Pattern:** Implements ASGI middleware following the callable pattern (not inheriting BaseHTTPMiddleware)
- **Purpose:** Provides cleanup/resource management after request completion, specifically for closing files after dependencies are closed
- **Context variable name:** Defaults to `"fastapi_middleware_astack"` and can be customized
- **Signature:** `__init__(app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack")`
- **Call signature:** `async __call__(scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None`
- **Behavior:** Creates an `AsyncExitStack` context manager and stores it in `scope[context_name]` so other code can register cleanup callbacks

### 5. How Middleware is Integrated into FastAPI

**Location in application:** `/fastapi/applications.py` contains the middleware orchestration

**Key integration points:**

1. **FastAPI class inheritance:**
   - `class FastAPI(Starlette)` - inherits Starlette's middleware infrastructure

2. **AsyncExitStackMiddleware integration:**
   - Imported at top: `from fastapi.middleware.asyncexitstack import AsyncExitStackMiddleware`
   - Automatically added in `build_middleware_stack()` method (lines 1022-1070)

3. **Middleware stack construction (lines 1035-1070):**
   ```python
   middleware = (
       [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]
       + self.user_middleware  # User-added middleware via add_middleware()
       + [
           Middleware(ExceptionMiddleware, handlers=exception_handlers, debug=debug),
           Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),  # FastAPI-specific
       ]
   )
   
   app = self.router
   for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
       app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
   return app
   ```

**Middleware execution order:**
- ServerErrorMiddleware (outermost - handles server errors)
- User middlewares (added via `app.add_middleware()` in LIFO order)
- ExceptionMiddleware (handles exceptions)
- AsyncExitStackMiddleware (innermost - resource cleanup)
- Router (application logic)

**Request flow:** ServerErrorMiddleware → User Middlewares → ExceptionMiddleware → AsyncExitStackMiddleware → Router
**Response flow:** Router → AsyncExitStackMiddleware → ExceptionMiddleware → User Middlewares → ServerErrorMiddleware

### 6. Middleware Class Interaction Patterns

**Two main patterns in FastAPI/Starlette:**

#### Pattern A: ASGI Callable Middleware (like AsyncExitStackMiddleware)
```python
class MyMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, ...config...):
        self.app = app
    
    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        # Pre-processing
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
        # Post-processing
```
- Low-level direct ASGI access
- Full control over request/response
- Used for custom middleware with HTTPException handling

#### Pattern B: BaseHTTPMiddleware (used by @app.middleware("http") decorator)
```python
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware

class MyHTTPMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        # Pre-processing
        response = await call_next(request)
        # Post-processing
        return response
```
- Higher-level Request/Response objects
- Automatic wrapping via decorator: `@app.middleware("http")`
- Implementation in FastAPI: line 4699 of applications.py wraps user functions in `BaseHTTPMiddleware`

### 7. Using Middleware in FastAPI

**Method 1: Using @app.middleware() decorator**
```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(request: Request, call_next):
    response = await call_next(request)
    return response
```

**Method 2: Using app.add_middleware() method**
```python
app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=["*"])
app.add_middleware(GZipMiddleware, minimum_size=1000)
```

**Method 3: Custom ASGI middleware class**
```python
class CustomMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
    
    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        # Custom processing
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)

app.add_middleware(CustomMiddleware, max_content_size=256)
```

### 8. Starlette Middleware Classes Available via FastAPI

| Middleware | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|
| `CORSMiddleware` | Cross-Origin Resource Sharing | `allow_origins`, `allow_credentials`, `allow_methods`, `allow_headers`, `expose_headers` |
| `GZipMiddleware` | GZip compression | `minimum_size` (default 500), `compresslevel` (1-9, default 9) |
| `HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` | Enforce HTTPS/WSS | None (simple toggle) |
| `TrustedHostMiddleware` | Validate Host header | `allowed_hosts` (list), `www_redirect` (bool, default True) |
| `WSGIMiddleware` | Wrap WSGI applications | WSGI app instance |

### 9. Type Signatures and ASGI Contract

**ASGI app interface:**
```python
type ASGIApp = Callable[[Scope, Receive, Send], Awaitable[None]]
```

**ASGI types (from starlette.types):**
- `Scope` - Dict containing connection metadata (path, method, headers, etc.)
- `Receive` - Async callable that returns messages from client
- `Send` - Async callable to send messages to client

**Middleware must implement ASGI:**
```python
async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
    # 1. Check scope["type"] == "http" or "websocket"
    # 2. Optionally wrap receive/send
    # 3. Call await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

### 10. File Paths for Reference

| File Path | Content | Type |
|-----------|---------|------|
| `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` | Middleware namespace/exports | Re-export |
| `/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py` | FastAPI's custom middleware | Implementation |
| `/fastapi/middleware/cors.py` | CORS re-export | Re-export |
| `/fastapi/middleware/gzip.py` | GZip re-export | Re-export |
| `/fastapi/middleware/httpsredirect.py` | HTTPS redirect re-export | Re-export |
| `/fastapi/middleware/trustedhost.py` | Trusted host re-export | Re-export |
| `/fastapi/middleware/wsgi.py` | WSGI re-export | Re-export |
| `/fastapi/applications.py` | FastAPI class & middleware integration | Core |

### Summary

FastAPI's middleware module is a **thin convenience wrapper** around Starlette's middleware system. It provides 5 re-exported Starlette middlewares (CORS, GZip, HTTPSRedirect, TrustedHost, WSGI) and implements one custom middleware (`AsyncExitStackMiddleware`) for resource cleanup. All middleware follows the ASGI specification with `async __call__(scope, receive, send)` pattern, and FastAPI uses Starlette's `BaseHTTPMiddleware` under the hood for the decorator-based `@app.middleware("http")` syntax.

from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()

router = APIRouter()


class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
    Args:
      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
    """

    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
        received = 0

        async def inner():
            nonlocal received
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                return message  # pragma: no cover

            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
            received += body_len
            if received > self.max_content_size:
                raise HTTPException(
                    422,
                    detail={
                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                        "code": 999,
                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
                    },
                )
            return message

        return inner

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)


@router.post("/middleware")
def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
    return {"message": "OK"}


app.include_router(router)
app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)


client = TestClient(app)


def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
    default_pydantic_max_size = 2**16
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (default_pydantic_max_size + 1))

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
        assert response.json() == {
            "detail": {
                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                "code": 999,
                "message": "File limit exceeded",
            }
        }


def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"<file content>")

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}

from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from docs_src.cors.tutorial001_py310 import app


def test_cors():
    client = TestClient(app)
    # Test pre-flight response
    headers = {
        "Origin": "https://localhost.tiangolo.com",
        "Access-Control-Request-Method": "GET",
        "Access-Control-Request-Headers": "X-Example",
    }
    response = client.options("/", headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    assert response.text == "OK"
    assert (
        response.headers["access-control-allow-origin"]
        == "https://localhost.tiangolo.com"
    )
    assert response.headers["access-control-allow-headers"] == "X-Example"

    # Test standard response
    headers = {"Origin": "https://localhost.tiangolo.com"}
    response = client.get("/", headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    assert response.json() == {"message": "Hello World"}
    assert (
        response.headers["access-control-allow-origin"]
        == "https://localhost.tiangolo.com"
    )

    # Test non-CORS response
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    assert response.json() == {"message": "Hello World"}
    assert "access-control-allow-origin" not in response.headers

- Middleware tests define the app inline or import from `docs_src/`
- Example test from `tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py` defines a custom ASGI middleware inline, creates app, adds middleware via `app.add_middleware(MyMiddleware, param=value)`, creates `client = TestClient(app)`, then tests

### Custom ASGI middleware pattern (from tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py):
```python
class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
```

```python
if client:
    host, port = client
```

## Requirements for RateLimitMiddleware

1. New file `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` implementing `RateLimitMiddleware`
2. Follows ASGI middleware pattern (pure `__call__` pattern, NOT BaseHTTPMiddleware)
3. Configuration: `requests_per_minute` (int, default 60), `burst_size` (int, default 10)
4. Token bucket algorithm for rate limiting, per-client IP address
5. Returns HTTP 429 with JSON body `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <seconds>}` when limit exceeded
6. Adds headers to ALL responses:
   - `X-RateLimit-Limit`: the requests_per_minute value
   - `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: remaining requests in current window
   - `X-RateLimit-Reset`: seconds until the bucket refills
7. Export from `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`
8. Test app at `tests/test_apps/test_rate_limit_app.py`
9. All existing tests must still pass

## Design Questions to Address

1. **Token Bucket Algorithm**: How to implement it correctly:
   - Each client IP gets a bucket
   - Bucket starts full at `burst_size` tokens
   - Tokens refill at `requests_per_minute / 60` tokens per second
   - Each request consumes 1 token
   - If no tokens available, return 429

2. **Response header injection**: Since we're using pure ASGI, we need to wrap the `send` callable to intercept the `http.response.start` message and inject headers before passing it downstream.

3. **State management**: Dictionary of per-IP buckets. Need to consider cleanup of stale entries (old IPs that haven't made requests in a while).

4. **Thread safety**: asyncio is single-threaded per event loop, but we should still use appropriate data structures.

5. **How to send 429 response**: Directly send `http.response.start` and `http.response.body` ASGI messages without calling `self.app`.

6. **X-RateLimit-Remaining calculation**: After consuming a token, what's remaining? After refill calculation.

7. **X-RateLimit-Reset**: Seconds until bucket is full again. If bucket is not full, compute time to refill to capacity.

Please design a detailed implementation plan covering:
- The token bucket data structure
- The middleware class with exact method signatures
- How to intercept and modify response headers via the `send` wrapper
- How to send the 429 response directly
- The export in __init__.py
- The test app structure and test cases
- Edge cases (no client IP, websocket connections, etc.)

  ```python
  from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
  from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
  
  class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
      def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
          self.app = app
          self.context_name = context_name
  
      async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
          async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
              scope[self.context_name] = stack
              await self.app(scope, receive, send)
  ```
